message:
Add CLI command structure with TUI and Index subcommands

Implement clap-based command-line interface defining application entry
points and subcommand routing, replacing the empty run() placeholder
with actual command dispatch logic.

Cli struct definition (clap derive macros):
Top-level argument parser with --db flag for custom database path
override (defaults to platform data directory computed by directories
crate in future config module) and required subcommand selector. Uses
#[derive(Parser)] macro for declarative CLI definition with minimal
boilerplate compared to builder pattern.

Metadata attributes:
- name: "coding-agent-search" as canonical binary name (matches
  Cargo.toml package name)
- version: Automatically populated from Cargo.toml version field via
  clap::crate_version!() expansion (currently "0.1.0"), displayed by
  --version flag
- about: Short description for --help output, aligned with README.md
  project summary

Commands enum (subcommand variants):
Define two initial subcommands per PLAN § 3.1 CLI entrypoint:

1. Tui: Launch interactive TUI (default subcommand when no args provided,
   future commit will add #[command(default_subcommand)] attribute).
   Delegates to ui::tui::run_tui() which initializes crossterm terminal,
   enters alternate screen, runs event loop until 'q' quit (implemented
   in next commit).

2. Index: Run indexer for building/updating search database (stub
   implementation logging only). Accepts --full flag for full rebuild
   vs incremental scan (default). Future implementation will:
   - Read config to determine enabled agents
   - Instantiate connectors (CodexConnector, ClineConnector, etc.)
   - For each enabled agent: call detect(), if found call scan()
   - Batch insert normalized conversations to SQLite via
     storage::SqliteStorage::insert_conversation_tree
   - Update Tantivy index from newly inserted messages
   - Display progress via indicatif::ProgressBar ("Indexing Codex:
     327/1024 sessions...")

run() function implementation:
Parse CLI args via Cli::parse() (reads std::env::args(), exits with
help message or error if invalid args), then match on command variant:
- Commands::Tui: Forward to ui::tui::run_tui(), propagate Result to
  main for error display (TUI errors already handled internally via
  terminal restoration on panic)
- Commands::Index { full }: Log stub message with target:"index" for
  filtering via RUST_LOG=index env var, conditionally log "full rebuild"
  vs "incremental run" based on --full flag presence

Target labels for structured logging:
Use tracing::info!(target: "index", ...) instead of bare info!() to
enable per-module log filtering. User can set RUST_LOG=coding_agent_search::index=debug
to see detailed indexing logs while suppressing UI event loop noise,
or RUST_LOG=info for balanced output. Target matches module path by
convention (index target for indexer module, even though currently
logged from lib.rs stub).

--db flag semantics (future):
Optional database path override allows:
- Testing with isolated database (--db /tmp/test.db) without affecting
  user's production index
- Multi-profile setups (--db ~/work-agents.db vs ~/personal-agents.db)
- Explicit path for reproducibility in bug reports ("please run with
  --db /tmp/repro.db and share the file")

If --db not provided, config module will compute default path using
directories::ProjectDirs (e.g., ~/.local/share/coding-agent-search/
agent_search.db on Linux) per PLAN § 11.1 configuration.

Subcommand expansion roadmap (PLAN § 3.1):
Additional subcommands planned for v1/v2:
- inspect <agent> <session-id>: Dump normalized view of single
  conversation in JSON format for debugging connector parsing
- export --agent <slug> --format <jsonl|csv>: Export conversations
  to portable format for backup or external analysis
- config: Interactive TUI for editing config.toml, toggling agents,
  setting theme (alternative to manual config file editing)
- stats: Display index statistics (conversation count per agent, total
  messages, index size, last scan timestamp)

Error propagation strategy:
Both TUI and Index subcommands return anyhow::Result, which run()
propagates to main(). main() uses the ? operator to surface errors to
user as formatted output (anyhow formats error chain with context).
TUI errors are rare (terminal initialization failure, panic during
render) but Index errors are expected (connector parse failures, disk
full during SQLite write) so descriptive error messages critical.

Integration with main.rs:
main() calls coding_agent_search::run().await (tokio async main),
which parses CLI and dispatches. If TUI/Index return Err, main()
terminates with non-zero exit code and prints error via anyhow's
Display impl. If Ok, main() returns Ok(()) and process exits 0.

This commit establishes CLI skeleton—actual TUI event loop and indexer
orchestration implemented in subsequent commits (TUI next, indexer
after connectors provide real scan() implementations per PLAN § 14.3
minimal TUI before indexer).

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (CLI
component), Cargo.toml (clap dependency with derive feature), main.rs
(run() invocation), ui/tui.rs (run_tui implementation in next commit).
